    "R1.1": "Artifact explicitly stakes three enumerated contributions; any review must check whether each is coherently defined and defensible as a distinct contribution — e.g., the 'novel uncertainty calibration' claim is never mechanistically grounded in this section despite being listed.",
    "R1.2": "The routing logic contains verifiable internal consistency obligations: τ selection by validation ROC sweep is described but the sensitivity target is never stated; the future-work paragraph references 'co-occurrence priors mentioned in Section 1' while no such priors appear in the presented section, creating a forward-reference inconsistency.",
    "R1.3": "Quantitative claims are concrete and checkable against the stated experimental setup: AUROC 0.889 matched 'within 0.004', 31% escalation, 4.2→1.6s cost reduction, and five-pathology coverage out of the 14-dimensional output vector — the mismatch between output dimensionality and reported pathology count is an alignment flag.",

rationale.R1.1: Artifact explicitly stakes three enumerated contributions; any review must check whether each is coherently defined and defensible as a distinct contribution — e.g., the 'novel uncertainty calibration' claim is never mechanistically grounded in this section despite being listed.
rationale.R1.2: The routing logic contains verifiable internal consistency obligations: τ selection by validation ROC sweep is described but the sensitivity target is never stated; the future-work paragraph references 'co-occurrence priors mentioned in Section 1' while no such priors appear in the presented section, creating a forward-reference inconsistency.
rationale.R1.3: Quantitative claims are concrete and checkable against the stated experimental setup: AUROC 0.889 matched 'within 0.004', 31% escalation, 4.2→1.6s cost reduction, and five-pathology coverage out of the 14-dimensional output vector — the mismatch between output dimensionality and reported pathology count is an alignment flag.
rationale.R1.6: The bare delegation 'Review this section' almost always includes writing quality; several phrasings warrant attention: 'modest gains' in the final sentence is unquantified, 'averaged over CPU + cloud GPU time' conflates heterogeneous hardware costs without clarification, and 'recover most of the benefit' is vague relative to the precision of surrounding claims.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical content — validating claim logic, evidence alignment, and contribution scope — or editorial writing quality and clarity?
